questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Regarding United Nations Security Council Resolution 1540 (UNSCR 1540), which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: It is a voluntary guideline for states to prevent the proliferation of nuclear weapons.
It is a legally binding resolution that aims to prevent non-state actors from acquiring WMDs.
India has officially opposed the resolution due to its impact on civil nuclear energy.
It specifically mandates the complete disarmament of all nuclear-armed states by 2030.

questions.explanation: UNSCR 1540 (2004) is a legally binding resolution that requires all UN member states to adopt and enforce measures to prevent non-state actors from acquiring nuclear, chemical, or biological weapons and their delivery systems. India supports this resolution.
questions.takeaway: UNSCR 1540 is a critical, legally binding tool in the global effort to keep weapons of mass destruction out of the hands of non-state actors.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: UNSCR 1540 (2004): Legally binding resolution preventing non-state actors from acquiring nuclear, chemical, biological weapons and their delivery systems. India Status: Supports Resolutions.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the functional distinction between the International Convention for the Suppression of Acts of Nuclear Terrorism (ICSANT) and the Convention on the Physical Protection of Nuclear Material (CPPNM)?

options.text: ICSANT focuses on the reduction of strategic nuclear warheads, while CPPNM focuses on the elimination of fissile material stockpiles.
ICSANT criminalizes acts of nuclear terrorism and promotes judicial cooperation, while CPPNM focuses on the physical security of nuclear material during transport and in facilities.
ICSANT is a non-binding high-level forum for heads of state, while CPPNM is a legally binding resolution targeting non-state actors.
ICSANT regulates the peaceful use of nuclear energy in space, while CPPNM prohibits the production of highly enriched uranium.

questions.explanation: ICSANT is designed to criminalize nuclear terrorism and facilitate police/judicial cooperation. In contrast, CPPNM (1979) is an international convention specifically for the physical protection of nuclear material during transport and within facilities.
questions.takeaway: While both relate to nuclear security, ICSANT is legal/punitive regarding terrorism, whereas CPPNM is technical/protective regarding material handling.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: ICSANT (2005): Criminalizes acts of nuclear terrorism and promotes police/judicial cooperation... CPPNM (1979): International convention for physical protection of nuclear material during transport and facilities.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following organizations or coalitions is primarily responsible for promoting the implementation of the Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons (TPNW)?

options.text: International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA)
Conference on Disarmament (CD)
International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons (ICAN)
Nuclear Security Summits (NSS)

questions.explanation: ICAN is a coalition of non-governmental organizations specifically dedicated to promoting adherence to and the implementation of the TPNW. India has not signed this treaty.
questions.takeaway: ICAN is the NGO-led driver behind the TPNW, distinguishing it from intergovernmental bodies like the IAEA.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons (ICAN): Coalition of non-governmental organizations promoting adherence to and implementation of the Treaty on the Prohibition of Nuclear Weapons (TPNW). India Status: Not Signed.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Regarding the Nuclear Security Summits (NSS), which of the following statements is most accurate?

options.text: They are the primary legal body for negotiating bilateral arms reduction between the US and Russia.
They are high-level global forums focused specifically on the prevention of nuclear terrorism.
They are a permanent UN subcommittee established by the Outer Space Treaty.
They are the technical wing of the IAEA responsible for civil nuclear cooperation.

questions.explanation: The Nuclear Security Summits (NSS) are described as high-level global forums specifically focused on the prevention of nuclear terrorism. India is a participant.
questions.takeaway: NSS is a security-focused forum (terrorism prevention) rather than a disarmament negotiation body or a technical agency.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Nuclear Security Summits (NSS): High-level global forums focused on preventing nuclear terrorism. India Status: Participant.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The 'Fat Man' and 'Little Boy' atomic bombs, which led to the establishment of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in 1957, were dropped on which two cities respectively?

options.text: Hiroshima and Nagasaki
Nagasaki and Hiroshima
Tokyo and Hiroshima
Nagasaki and Tokyo

questions.explanation: According to the text, 'Little Boy' struck Hiroshima on August 6 and 'Fat Man' struck Nagasaki on August 9. Therefore, 'Fat Man' and 'Little Boy' correspond to Nagasaki and Hiroshima respectively.
questions.takeaway: Precision in historical facts regarding the atomic bombings is often tested in the context of the subsequent creation of the IAEA.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: On August 6 and 9, 1945, atomic bombs ‘Little Boy’ and ‘Fat Man’ struck Hiroshima and Nagasaki respectively... International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) was established in 1957.
